message:
[medium] linear: reject api_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_api_url_for_auth pinned the host to api.linear.app in
direct-api_key mode and accepted any URL in credential_id mode, but
returned parsed.to_string() verbatim — query, fragment, and userinfo
components survived into LinearClient's downstream URL construction.
A configure payload like
    {"api_key": "lin_x", "api_url": "https://api.linear.app?leak=x"}
would pass validation and then leak `?leak=x` on every Linear
GraphQL request (with the endpoint path appended after the `?`
boundary). Userinfo (`https://attacker:pw@api.linear.app/...`)
would bake into every URL and silently override the api_key the
connector sets via the Authorization header.

Completes the "pin api-key origin" trust gate shipped in a763ca66
by closing the three URL-component escape hatches the host-pin
left open. Same pattern as asana / airtable / gmail / notion /
telegram / discord / whatsapp landed this session.

- validate_api_url_for_auth now rejects userinfo and query/fragment
  components before the host-allowlist / scheme-allowlist checks,
  in both ApiKey and CredentialId modes.
- Six new unit tests: accept clean api.linear.app/graphql,
  ApiKey reject (query / fragment / userinfo), CredentialId reject
  (query), and a substring-smuggle regression verifying
  `https://evil.com/api.linear.app/graphql` is rejected because the
  parsed host is evil.com.

Verification: `cargo check -p fcp-linear` under CARGO_TARGET_DIR=
/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cannot complete because fcp-crypto fails to build
due to a committed indentation regression in crates/fcp-crypto/src/
hpke_seal.rs:366 (same blocker surfaced in asana/airtable commits).
Linear diff reviewed manually: additions are std url::Url accessor
calls returning standard shapes, error shapes identical to surrounding
validator branches. Variants LinearAuth::ApiKey and
LinearAuth::CredentialId verified present at connector.rs:60-61.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
